assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Code Inspection Analysis' section references all 4 files: advisory/endpoints/get.rs (handler signature, 404 pattern), advisory/service/advisory.rs (method patterns, error wrapping), advisory/model/summary.rs (severity field for aggregation), and common/src/error.rs (AppError variants, .context() pattern).

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists exactly 3 files to modify (model/mod.rs, service/advisory.rs, endpoints/mod.rs) and 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs test). server/src/main.rs is noted as 'no changes needed'. All are within scope.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: Files 4, 5, 6 in the plan are: modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs (SeveritySummary struct), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs (GET handler), and tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (integration tests). All 3 are present.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: Files 1, 2, 3 in the plan are: advisory/model/mod.rs (add pub mod severity_summary), advisory/service/advisory.rs (add severity_summary method), and advisory/endpoints/mod.rs (register route and module). All 3 are present.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit message is 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM dashboards' with 'Refs: TC-9201' in the footer. This follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'advisory', and TC-9201 in the footer.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit command explicitly includes --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' at the end of the git commit command.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: The Branch Operations section shows 'git checkout main' followed by 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. The Target Branch Extraction section states 'The task specifies Target Branch: main. All branch operations will use main as the base.'

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md identifies both: 'Error Handling Conventions' section describes 'AppError variants' and '.context("message")?' pattern; Service/Endpoint/Model conventions sections describe the three-layer module structure with model/, service/, endpoints/ directories.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: The first section is 'Target Branch Extraction' which states: 'The task specifies Target Branch: main. All branch operations will use main as the base.'

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Description Digest Verification (Step 1.5)' section states: 'No digest comment found on TC-9201. Per shared/description-digest-protocol.md, when no digest comment is present, proceed with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility). Log warning: No description digest found for TC-9201 — proceeding under backward-compatibility allowance.'
